//: Register mt5-trading-mcp with OpenClaw. Merge this 'mcp.servers' entry into ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json (JSON5). NOTE the key is 'mcp.servers' (top-level 'mcp', nested 'servers') - NOT 'mcpServers' as some other clients use. stdio is implied by 'command' (there is no stdio 'transport' field; for HTTP, drop command/args and add "url": "http://localhost:8765/mcp" + "transport": "streamable-http" after starting `python -m mt5_mcp serve --transport http`). CLI equivalent: openclaw mcp set mt5-mcp '{"command":"python","args":["-m","mt5_mcp","serve"]}'. TOOL SCOPING: OpenClaw has no per-server read-only tool filter (unlike the Hermes 'include' list or Codex 'enabled_tools'), so the 4 mutating tools (place_order / modify_order / cancel_order / close_position) stay gated by mt5-mcp's OWN in-process consent engine. For a harness-level block, add their prefixed names (mt5-mcp__place_order, mt5-mcp__modify_order, mt5-mcp__cancel_order, mt5-mcp__close_position) to gateway.tools.deny - but per the docs that only covers the HTTP /tools/invoke path, so the cleanest hard read-only is a second mt5-mcp instance exposing read tools only. Confirm field names against https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/mcp.
